hooks.prompt: Review this file change for security issues. Check for: hardcoded secrets/API keys, SQL injection, command injection via os/exec with user input, path traversal, use of unsafe package, weak crypto (MD5/SHA1 for security), unbounded allocations from external input, goroutine leaks (missing context cancellation), information disclosure in error messages. If ANY security issue is found, respond with decision:block and explain. Otherwise allow. Tool input: $ARGUMENTS

Check if this bash command is safe for a Go project. Block if it: deletes files outside the project, modifies system files, installs packages globally without user intent, runs curl/wget piped to shell, uses force flags on git destructively (push --force to main, reset --hard without cause), or could leak credentials. Allow standard Go toolchain commands, git read operations, file inspection, and .claude/hooks/ scripts. Command: $ARGUMENTS

hooks.command: f=$(jq -r '.tool_response.filePath // .tool_input.file_path' 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$f" ] && echo "$f" | grep -qE '\.go$'; then gofmt -l "$f" 2>/dev/null | head -1 | { read -r line; if [ -n "$line" ]; then gofmt -w "$f" 2>/dev/null; echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PostToolUse","additionalContext":"Auto-formatted with gofmt"}}'; fi; }; fi

hooks.prompt: The subagent just finished. Verify: (1) Did it complete ALL assigned tasks from its dispatch prompt? (2) Did it run tests if it wrote code? (3) Did it note any corrections or new patterns discovered? (4) Did it use the 3-step retrieval protocol (index.md, wiki-query.sh, targeted files)? If any item is missing or incomplete, respond with decision:block and list what's missing. Otherwise respond with decision:approve.

hooks.command: changed=$(git diff --name-only 2>/dev/null | grep -E '\.go$' | head -20); if [ -n "$changed" ]; then (go vet ./... 2>&1 | head -20) || true; fi
hooks.statusMessage: Running go vet on modified Go files...
